participants.name: Jalon Daniels
participants.notes: NFL Combine invitee. Measured 6-1, 219 at combine with 30 3/8" arms and 9 5/8" hands. Ran 4.65 40 at combine. Did not re-test drills at pro day per PFF; threw passes to receivers and did positional work. PFF lists pro day weight at 224 lbs.

participants.name: Bryce Foster
participants.notes: Transfer from Texas A&M. Full pro day workout. PFF Big Board Rank: 294. 10-yard split: 1.95, 20-yard split: 3.17 per PFF.

participants.name: Emmanuel Henderson Jr.
participants.notes: NFL Combine invitee. Ran 4.44 40 at combine with 35" vertical and 120" broad jump. At pro day, ran shuttle (4.33) and 3-cone (7.34) per PFF. Measurements listed are from pro day. Transfer from Alabama.

participants.name: Enrique Cruz Jr.
participants.notes: NFL Combine invitee. Ran 4.94 40 at combine (313 lbs) with 35" vertical, 9'8" broad jump, 33 3/4" arms. Pro day measurements from PFF: 6-5 1/4, 310, 34 7/8" arms, 10 3/8" hands, 82 5/8" wingspan. Participated in positional drills at pro day. Transfer from Syracuse.

participants.name: Justice Finkley
participants.notes: Participated in pro day including 40-yard dash (ran two attempts per photo evidence) and positional drills. Specific numerical results not publicly reported outside of KU Pro Day Book PDF. Transfer from Auburn.

participants.name: Dean Miller
participants.notes: PFF Big Board Rank: 350. PFF reports he 'Did not test' at Kansas pro day.

participants.name: Kobe Baynes
participants.notes: Participated in pro day including positional drills. Previously at American Bowl. Specific numerical results not publicly reported outside of KU Pro Day Book PDF. Listed with ~81.5" wingspan per draft profile.

participants.notes: NFL Combine Specialist Showcase invitee. Previously at Hula Bowl. Converted 50- and 55-yard field goals during pro day workout. Kicking specialist; did not participate in standard combine-style drills.

additional_notes: Kansas held its 2026 Pro Day on Wednesday, March 11, at the Anderson Family Football Complex in Lawrence, Kansas. This was KU's first on-campus Pro Day since 2023, after the Big 12 had experimented with centralized, leaguewide Pro Day events in Frisco, Texas the previous two years. All 32 NFL teams were represented. The event began with measurements, bench press, and vertical jump in the weight room, then transitioned to the Indoor Complex for on-field drills (40-yard dash, broad jump, 3-cone, shuttle, and positional work). Four Jayhawks — Jalon Daniels, Emmanuel Henderson Jr., Enrique Cruz Jr., and Laith Marjan — had previously attended the NFL Combine. Daniels focused on throwing/positional work at pro day rather than re-testing athletic drills. Henderson added shuttle (4.33) and 3-cone (7.34) at pro day after only running 40/vertical/broad at the combine. Dean Miller (EDGE, PFF #350) was listed as a participant but PFF reports he did not test. The KU Athletics official Pro Day Book PDF (https://kuathletics.com/documents/2026/3/11/Pro_Day_Book.pdf) contains the complete results for all 23 participants but the detailed numerical data within the PDF could not be extracted through web search — only PFF-tracked prospects (Foster, Henderson, Cruz, Miller, Daniels) had individual results published in publicly accessible articles. The timing method (electronic vs. hand-timed) was not specified in available sources. The event was held indoors.
